prompt: Add Server-Sent Events endpoint for live notifications

turns.content:
<task>
Add a Server-Sent Events (SSE) endpoint for streaming live notifications to the frontend.
</task>

1	 from flask import Flask
2	 from api.routes import api_bp
3	 
4	 app = Flask(__name__)
5	 app.register_blueprint(api_bp)

arguments.query: Flask Server-Sent Events SSE streaming response generator Python

turns.content:
1. Flask SSE - https://flask.palletsprojects.com/en/3.0.x/patterns/streaming/
   Use Response with generator and content_type='text/event-stream'.
2. SSE Format - https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events
   Format: 'data: {json}\n\n'. Optional id and event fields.

arguments.title: Server-Sent Events (SSE) format
arguments.content:
Content-Type: text/event-stream
Format: 'data: <text>

' for each event
Optional fields: 'id: <id>
', 'event: <type>
', 'retry: <ms>
'
Flask: Response(generator(), content_type='text/event-stream')
Client: new EventSource('/api/events'); source.onmessage = fn

arguments.message: SSE endpoint is ready at GET /api/events. Use `publish_event('notification', {'message': '...'})` from anywhere in the backend to push events to all connected clients. I included a 30-second keepalive to prevent proxy timeouts. On the frontend: `new EventSource('/api/events')`.

arguments.summary: Created SSE endpoint with in-memory event bus. Supports named events, keepalive, and handles slow/dead clients by dropping events from full queues.
arguments.final_answer: SSE streaming endpoint at GET /api/events. In-memory pub/sub with queue per client. 30-second keepalive. publish_event() broadcasts to all connected clients.
